etymology.description: semantic 氵(水) (shuǐ, "water") + phonetic 十(十) (shí) -> a liquid
etymology.interpretation: The semantic component 氵(水) (shuǐ, "water") suggests a liquid, while the phonetic component 十(十) (shí) provides the sound 'zhī'. Together, they represent a liquid substance, hence the meaning 'juice'.

examples.chinese: 我喜欢喝橙汁(我喜歡喝橙汁)。
examples.english: I like to drink orange juice.

examples.chinese: 她把苹果榨成汁(她把蘋果榨成汁)。
examples.pinyin: Tā bǎ píngguǒ zhà chéng zhī.

examples.chinese: 果汁比汽水健康(果汁比汽水健康)。
